_note: FRED forward release-date schedule — recorded once via the u43 follow-up fixture procedure. The ``release/dates?release_id=N&include_release_dates_with_no_data=true&sort_order=desc`` shape returns future scheduled release dates first (FRED publishes the announcement schedule even before data lands; ``date`` rows beyond the most-recent observation date are forward-looking). Tests replay these bodies via httpx.MockTransport (R10). Re-record only when the live shape changes; document the change in aidlc-docs/audit.md. The api_key was masked to ``***`` in the URL template above; bodies are byte-equal to the live response and contain no key. The byte-equal contract is enforced by the sha256 hashes below.

empty_far_future.json._note: Recorded with realtime_start=2099-01-01 — FRED returns count=0 / empty release_dates array. Used to assert the adapter returns an empty list rather than raising.

invalid_key.json._note: Recorded with api_key=invalidkey. FRED returns HTTP 400 with an error_code/error_message body describing the required api_key shape. The literal string ``api_key`` appears in FRED's error message text; this is upstream prose, not a leaked credential.
